Petition To Determine Existence Of Trust And To Impose Constructive Trust
Off Calendar Sep-26-2017 Continued To Nov-07-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Dan Lassen for Diana Buteau.
Thomas Walsh appeared by telephone for Peter Miller (Beneficiary).
Arbella Azizian appeared by telephone for Debra Sanchez.

The Court noted that there is a motion to enforce settlement and for Diana to remain on as Trustee that is scheduled to be heard on November 1st, stated that it received a report indicating that Diana is not meeting her duties and asked where the parties stand. Mr. Lassen stated that he filed his motion to withdraw because of a serious lack of communication with his Client, that he received a call yesterday from an Attorney who is going to take over and asked the Court to grant his motion. The Court informed Mr. Lassen that it is not inclined to grant the motion until a substitution of attorney is filed. Ms. Azizian stated that they have not had contact from anybody, that they are asking for Diana to be removed, that the only reason the proceeds from the sale are secure is because they demanded the funds be placed in a blocked account, that proceeds from sale of the coin collection is in Diana's hands, that Diana is being uncooperative and that it is not conducive to administration of the Trust. The Court stated that there are procedural issues, that the report or the motion to enforce the settlement agreement are not a proper mechanism to remove the Trustee, and that if the intent is to remove Ms. Buteau or appoint a Successor Trustee, they will need to file an amended petition or a new petition. The Court also stated that what is being requested is inconsistent with the relief sought in the pending petition and that it is not inclined to grant the motion to withdraw today. The Motion to Be Relieved as Counsel and the Petition to Determine Existence of Trust, etc. are continued to November 7, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to give Ms. Azizian the opportunity to figure out what she wants to do and for Ms. Buteau to figure her representation.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

Off Calendar Mar-14-2017 Continued To May-16-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Arbella Azizian, counsel for Debra Sanchez, beneficiary [Court Call]; John Minton, counsel for and with Diana Buteau, Trustee.

Ms. Azizian addresses the current posture of the litigation, noting that it would appear the parties are close to settlement of the status of the trusteeship. She notes there are other issues outstanding as does the Court with respect to the various issues raised in the Petition, including the administration of the trust.

Mr. Minton advises that the trusteeship issue is most likely close to settlement but would invite the Court to decide that issue at this time. While prepared to rule, The Court declines, advising counsel that the Court will require global resolution of all issues raised in the petition, whether by settlement, stipulation, judicial determination or a combination of those resolution mechanisms. Mr. Minton also notes that final resolution will require the determination of the value of his client's services with respect to the preparation of the home for sale and to what extent those services will be compensated.

After further consideration and at the request of counsel, the Court continues the hearing to May 16, 2017 at 9:00 a.m. and advises counsel that they must be prepared by that date to report to the Court the mechanisms by which the various issues in the Petition will be resolved.

Petition To Determine Existence Of Trust And To Impose Constructive Trust
Off Calendar Dec-13-2016 Continued To Feb-21-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Arbella Azizian for (petitioner) Debra Sanchez. Ronald Augustinsky for (respondent) Diana Buteau.

The Court stated that there is opposition to deal with who is controlling the trust and asked counsel if they have talked. Mr. Augustinsky stated that they qualify for pro bono mediation and are willing to participate. Ms. Azizian stated that they are willing to mediate, that there is mis-trust, that they will file a response to the objections and that there are allegations of liquidating assets and that the house is being cleared out. Mr. Augustinsky stated that the beneficiaries are wanting to take jewelry and personal property and that the items have been moved to storage for safekeeping. The Court asked counsel if his client is willing to agree that no property will be sold. Mr. Augustinsky replied yes, that the issue is the Lis Pendens that was filed and now they cannot sell and that the proceeds would go into a blocked account. The Court stated that the parties could spend a lot of time and money and that they need to talk. Ms. Azizian stated that the concern is the co-trusteeship and the matter of trust and transparency. The Court stated that the parties need to talk this out and that the fight will delay and deplete what there is at the end, that it does not know if the surviving spouse could give authority to make changes to the Bypass Trust terms including successor Trustees and suggested that the parties mediate or agree to a neutral trustee. Mr. Augustinsky stated that this is a small estate. The Court stated that it cannot be to anyone's benefit to spend all of the money to fight, that they should be able to talk things through and come to a solution, that it appreciates that no one will be selling property, that if everyone is in agreement regarding sale of the real property there is no reason not to get started and that it is in everyone's interest to distribute. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than February 7, 2017. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by February 10, 2017. The Petition to Determine Existence of Trust and to Impose Constructive Trust was continued to and the Parties are to return on February 21, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. The Court stated that if the parties reach an agreement they are to file the appropriate documents for relief and the court will try to facilitate it, if the issues are not resolved the parties are to talk about how to go forward and the Court will look for input from them. Ms. Azizian stated that they want an accounting. Mr. Augustinsky stated that they are planning to do an accounting at the end. The Court stated that counsel should provide information as to what there is, that it may facilitate the mediation, that it expects full information to be disclosed, that counsel may submit a proposed order regarding the sharing of information and not to sell any property pending the mediation and that the other issues in the petition are reserved.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Ideta. (Not Reported)
